changes.field: Intelligence Core analytics files — no output fingerprint backstop (Stage 2B)
changes.finding: Four permitted backend analytics files are Intelligence Core output-assembly components. Any change to them affects ALL results renders — not only the f2dcb58f test panel. The validation commands run deterministic test suites and regression guards, which catch named defect classes. However, no full analytical output fingerprint verification (equivalent to golden_gate_local.py or an AB baseline diff) is required post-modification. This leaves a gap: a subtle change to conditional assembly logic in narrative_report_compiler_v1.py could alter output for panels not covered by named defect-class tests. Risk mitigation: Cursor must treat all changes to these four files as exclusively string-substitution or boolean display-gate operations (e.g. 'if string contains X, replace with Y'; 'if field value equals Z, suppress label'). If any change would add a new conditional branch, modify scoring weighting, restructure output assembly, or introduce new logic paths, Cursor must STOP that sub-item and escalate. The prompt's forbidden-files constraint and existing Sentinel suite are the primary backstops. This note makes the constraint explicit for the executor.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Does not require prompt revision. Cursor must self-enforce the string-substitution-only rule for these four files.

changes.finding: Hardening was invoked as 'harden work_id: FE-R6A: LC-S18'. The active prompt has work_id: FE-R6A only. The ': LC-S18' suffix appears to be a stale reference to a previous sprint. Hardening applies to FE-R6A as written. No action required from Cursor.
